content:
# Your Fin Is a Glorified FAQ Bot. Here's How to Fix It.

![FAQ Bot vs Live Intelligence](/blog/fin-upgrade-hero.jpg)

You installed Intercom Fin. You uploaded your help articles. You trained it on your pricing page, your FAQ, your feature docs.

And it works — for exactly what you'd expect: surfacing answers to questions your customers could have found themselves if they scrolled long enough.

But then a prospect asks: **"How does your pricing compare to [competitor]?"**

Fin: "I'd recommend checking our pricing page for details on our plans."

Not helpful.

Then they ask: **"What companies like mine are using you?"**

Fin: "We work with a variety of businesses. I'd suggest speaking with our sales team for case studies."

Still not helpful.

The problem isn't Fin. The problem is Fin was built to retrieve. Not to know.

## What Fin Is Missing

Fin is an open-book exam where the book was written months ago. It retrieves from static content. It cannot:

- **Pull live company data** — Funding rounds, headcount growth, tech stack, recent news
- **Answer competitive questions** — Because your help center doesn't have competitor intel
- **Remember across conversations** — Every session starts from zero
- **Reason about causality** — Why did this company grow? What triggered that hiring spike?

These are the questions that determine whether a prospect becomes a customer. And Fin cannot answer them because the answers are not in your help center.

## The Fix: Forage

![How Forage connects to Fin](/blog/fin-upgrade-diagram.jpg)

Forage is a knowledge layer designed to plug into AI agents. It gives Fin three things it does not have:

### 1. Live Company Intelligence

Forage pulls real-time data from the web: company profiles, funding rounds, employee counts, technologies used, recent news. When a prospect asks about a specific company, Fin can answer from current data — not what was written last quarter.

### 2. Memory That Compounds

Every conversation feeds back into a persistent knowledge graph: 1 million entities deep. Companies, people, funding rounds, competitors, technologies, buying signals. When Fin talks to Prospect A about the FinTech vertical, it learns. When Prospect B comes in from the same vertical an hour later, Fin already knows the landscape.

Your help center is static. Forage is alive.

### 3. Causal Reasoning, Not Retrieval

Fin retrieves. Forage reasons.

Ask what's upstream of a company's expansion. Simulate market shifts. Trace contagion across competitors, supply chains, and hiring signals. Fin stops being a search bar and starts being an actual advisor.

This is the difference between a chatbot that reads your FAQ and an advisor that understands your market.

## How It Works

Forage runs as an MCP server. One Apify token. Works in Claude, n8n, GPT-4, any MCP client — including Intercom.

1. Fin receives a question it cannot answer from help articles alone
2. Forage pulls live intelligence from the relevant sources
3. The knowledge graph provides context from prior conversations
4. Fin responds with current, contextual, sourced intelligence
5. The conversation feeds back into the graph

No extra dashboards. No manual updates. The intelligence layer compounds automatically.

## The Before and After

**Before (Fin with help articles only):**

> Prospect: What's your competitive advantage?
>
> Fin: Forage differentiates through its knowledge graph and pay-per-call pricing model.

Vague. Generic. Could be any product's website.

**After (Fin powered by Forage):**

> Prospect: What's your competitive advantage?
>
> Fin: The main alternatives are [Company A] which focuses on RAG retrieval without a graph layer, [Company B] which offers agent memory but without live data feeds, and [Company C] which does web scraping but no causal reasoning. The gap most teams hit is that their agents reset every session. What specifically are you comparing against — memory, live data, or reasoning?

Specific. Competitive-aware. Turns the answer into a qualification question.

## Setup

[Sign up for Forage](https://useforage.xyz/#signup) — $5 free credits, no card.

Grab your Apify token.

Connect Forage as an MCP server to your Fin setup.

Done. Fin now has memory, live data, and reasoning.

## Want More Fin Users?

If you're reading this and already use Intercom Fin, we have an affiliate program. Every Fin user you send our way gets Forage credits. You get a cut.

[Sign up for the referral program here](https://app.intercom.com/admins/referral?referral_code=f4981688c801).

## Bottom Line

Fin is good at what it does: retrieving answers from content you've already written.

But the questions that close deals require live data, competitive context, and memory across conversations. That's what Forage adds.

[Dual-wield them](https://useforage.xyz/#signup). Your prospects will notice.

faqSchema.question: How does Forage integrate with Intercom Fin?
faqSchema.answer: Forage runs as an MCP server. Connect it to your Fin setup using one Apify token. Fin can then call Forage's 36 tools to pull live web data, query the knowledge graph, and return contextual answers instead of static help article responses.

faqSchema.question: What's the knowledge graph?
faqSchema.answer: A persistent graph of ~1 million entities: companies, people, funding rounds, competitors, technologies, buying signals. Every conversation feeds back into it. Fin learns from every session and applies that knowledge to future conversations.

faqSchema.question: Does Forage replace Intercom's help center?
faqSchema.answer: No. Forage augments Fin. Your help center handles common questions. Forage handles dynamic questions requiring current data, competitive context, or reasoning across entities.

faqSchema.question: How much does Forage cost?
faqSchema.answer: Pay per call. $5 free credits on signup, no credit card required. Most users spend $15-60/month depending on usage volume.

faqSchema.question: What's the referral program for Fin users?
faqSchema.answer: If you refer other Intercom Fin users to Forage, you earn credits for every signup. Sign up at the referral link to get your tracking code.
